mapped.summary:
ACE-Step 1.5's "Simple Mode brain" is a Qwen3-style causal LM family ("acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B/1.7B/4B"; 1.7B ships inside the main HF repo ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5, 0.6B/4B are separate ACE-Step/* repos). It is driven by LLMHandler (acestep/llm_inference.py) with three backends: nano-vllm (CUDA), PyTorch ("pt"), and mlx-lm ("mlx", preferred and auto-attempted on Apple Silicon even when "vllm" is requested). ACESTEP_INIT_LLM gates whether the LM loads at startup (auto = GPU-tier detection); when force-disabled, lazy loading is blocked and LM-dependent request fields hard-fail or are auto-disabled. --lm-model-path merely sets ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH. Per request (/release_task), the LM is required by thinking / sample_mode / sample_query / use_format / full_analysis_only, and optionally engaged by use_cot_caption / use_cot_language / implicit use_cot_metas; thinking=true makes the LM also generate the 5Hz audio codes (llm_dit). Plan-only "Enhance" exists as separate synchronous endpoints: POST /format_input (rewrite caption+lyrics+fill metas) and POST /v1/create_sample (query -> full plan), plus analysis_only=true on /release_task (LM metas only, no audio). Critical lyrics finding: in the default path user lyrics flow verbatim into DiT conditioning ("# Languages
{lang}

# Lyric
{lyrics}<|endoftext|>", truncated at 2048 tokens); no code path rewrites/filters them unless the client opts into use_format or sample_mode. The caption, however, IS rewritten by default because use_cot_caption defaults to true. No safety tuning, refusal logic, NSFW/profanity filtering, or content moderation exists anywhere in the request path; system prompts are one-line task instructions and constrained decoding is a purely structural FSM. LM sampling is exposed over REST via lm_temperature/lm_cfg_scale/lm_top_k/lm_top_p/lm_negative_prompt (lm_repetition_penalty and constrained_decoding are parsed but dead); max tokens is derived from duration, and the <think> CoT is structural, not a toggleable thinking mode.

claims.claim: The planner LM is the 'acestep-5Hz-lm' family in three sizes: 0.6B, 1.7B, 4B. HF repos: ACE-Step/acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B and ACE-Step/acestep-5Hz-lm-4B are standalone; acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B is bundled in the main repo ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5 and is DEFAULT_LM_MODEL. It is a Qwen3-style chat model (code references Qwen's chat template and mlx-lm's Qwen3 class; <think> CoT, <|im_end|> turns). Models are loaded from <checkpoints>/<lm_model_path>.

evidence.quote: LM_MODEL_NAMES = {"0.6B": "acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B", "1.7B": "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B", "4B": "acestep-5Hz-lm-4B"}
MAIN_MODEL_REPO = "ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5" ... "acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B": "ACE-Step/acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B", "acestep-5Hz-lm-4B": "ACE-Step/acestep-5Hz-lm-4B" ... DEFAULT_LM_MODEL = "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B"
if lm_model_path is None: lm_model_path = "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B" ... full_lm_model_path = os.path.join(checkpoint_dir, lm_model_path)
because Qwen's chat template renders assistant messages containing a </think> tag
the "model." prefix ... which is what mlx-lm's Qwen3 model expects

claims.topic: Backend selection (mlx vs pt vs nano-vllm)
claims.claim: Valid backends are {vllm, pt, mlx} (vllm = vendored nano-vllm in acestep/third_parts/nano-vllm, CUDA-only). In LLMHandler.initialize: if backend=='mlx' OR (backend=='vllm' AND device=='mps'), MLX is attempted first via mlx_lm; on failure it falls back to PyTorch. 'vllm' on a non-CUDA device silently becomes 'pt'. vllm also falls back to pt when a preflight warning fires or free VRAM < 2GB, and to MLX-then-pt if vllm init throws on MPS. On macOS/MPS the GPU config sets recommended_backend='mlx' and lm_backend_restriction='pt_mlx_only'.

evidence.quote: VALID_LM_BACKENDS = {"vllm", "pt", "mlx"}
if backend == "mlx" or (backend == "vllm" and device == "mps"): ... if backend == "vllm" and device != "cuda": ... backend = "pt"
vLLM disabled due to insufficient free VRAM ... falling back to PyTorch backend ... if device == "mps" and self._is_mlx_available(): ... trying MLX backend
from nanovllm import LLM, SamplingParams ... nano-vllm is not installed. Please install it using 'cd acestep/third_parts/nano-vllm && pip install .'
lm_backend_restriction="pt_mlx_only" if _mps ... recommended_backend="mlx" if _mps
_is_mlx_available ... import mlx_lm

claims.claim: At server startup the backend is resolved via resolve_lm_backend(os.getenv('ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND'), gpu_config): invalid/missing values fall back to the GPU config's recommended_backend ('mlx' on MPS, 'vllm' on CUDA tiers), then restrictions are applied (pt_only -> 'pt'; pt_mlx_only + vllm -> mlx/pt). On per-request lazy init the precedence is req.lm_backend > ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND > 'vllm', validated only against the literal set {vllm,pt,mlx}. The /format_input and /v1/create_sample lazy inits read only the env var (default 'vllm'). There is no --lm-backend CLI flag on the API server.

evidence.quote: lm_backend = resolve_lm_backend(os.getenv("ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND"), gpu_config)

def resolve_lm_backend(...): backend = (requested_backend or "").strip().lower(); if backend not in VALID_LM_BACKENDS: backend = recommended_backend ... if lm_backend_restriction == "pt_only": return "pt"
backend = (req.lm_backend or os.getenv("ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND") or "vllm").strip().lower(); if backend not in {"vllm", "pt", "mlx"}: backend = "vllm"
backend = os.getenv("ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND", "vllm").strip().lower(); if backend not in {"vllm", "pt", "mlx"}: backend = "vllm"
argparse flags are only --host, --port, --api-key, --download-source, --init-llm, --lm-model-path, --no-init (no backend flag)

claims.claim: Startup: unset or 'auto' -> use gpu_config.init_lm_default (GPU-tier auto-detect); truthy {1,true,yes,y,on} -> force-load LM; any other non-empty value -> force-disable. When disabled, app.state._llm_lazy_load_disabled=True, which permanently blocks per-request lazy init: requests requiring the LM error out, and use_cot_caption/use_cot_language are silently auto-disabled. The lazy-init helper additionally blocks if ACESTEP_INIT_LLM is in {0,false,no,n,off}. /format_input and /v1/create_sample return HTTP 503 'LLM not initialized. Set ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=true' when lazy load is disabled. --init-llm simply sets ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=true.

evidence.quote: init_llm_env = os.getenv("ACESTEP_INIT_LLM", "").strip().lower() ... if not init_llm_env or init_llm_env == "auto": ... elif init_llm_env in {"1", "true", "yes", "y", "on"}: ... else: init_llm = False
app.state._llm_lazy_load_disabled = True ... To enable LLM: Set ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=true ... Or use --init-llm command line flag
if getattr(app_state, "_llm_lazy_load_disabled", False): ... if init_llm_env in {"0", "false", "no", "n", "off"}: ... LLM disabled via ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=false
raise HTTPException(status_code=503, detail="LLM not initialized. Set ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=true in .env to enable.")
if args.init_llm: os.environ["ACESTEP_INIT_LLM"] = "true"

claims.claim: --lm-model-path only sets the ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH env var. At startup: env value wins; otherwise the GPU-tier recommended model is chosen (0.6B for low tiers, 1.7B for mid, 4B for unlimited); unsupported choices fall back to the recommended one. On per-request lazy init: req.lm_model_path > ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH > 'acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B'. Important for the TS client: per-request lm_model_path/lm_backend are honored ONLY when the LM has not yet been initialized — once loaded, those request fields are ignored (no reload).

evidence.quote: "--lm-model-path" ... if args.lm_model_path: os.environ["ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH"] = args.lm_model_path
lm_model_path = os.getenv("ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH", "").strip() ... recommended_lm = get_recommended_lm_model(gpu_config) ... Falling back to supported LM model

initialized = getattr(app_state, "_llm_initialized", False) ... if initialized or had_error is not None: return ... lm_model_path = (req.lm_model_path or os.getenv("ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH") or "acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B").strip()
tier configs: available_lm_models ["acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B"] ... recommended_lm_model "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B" ... "acestep-5Hz-lm-4B"

claims.topic: (a) When the LM runs — triggering request fields
claims.claim: On POST /release_task: the LM is REQUIRED when any of thinking, sample_mode, non-empty sample_query, use_format, or full_analysis_only is set (request fails if LM unavailable). It is OPTIONALLY engaged by use_cot_caption / use_cot_language (both default TRUE; silently auto-disabled if LM unavailable). sample_mode/sample_query triggers create_sample (query rewriting + lyric generation + metadata synthesis, replacing prompt/lyrics/metas wholesale; language/instrumental hints are regex-parsed from the query). use_format triggers format_sample (rewrites caption AND lyrics, fills metas). Inside generate_music, the LM additionally runs a CoT metadata phase for every text2music request whenever it is loaded (use_cot_metas = not sample_mode, not a REST field), EXCEPT: task types cover/cover-nofsq/repaint/extract skip the LM entirely, and the CoT phase is skipped when the user supplies all four of bpm+keyscale+timesignature+duration. thinking=true (REST default false) switches infer_type to 'llm_dit' so the LM also generates the 5Hz audio codes consumed by the DiT.

evidence.quote: require_llm = thinking or sample_mode or has_sample_query or use_format or full_analysis_only; want_llm = use_cot_caption or use_cot_language ... raise RuntimeError(f"5Hz LM init failed: ...")
if sample_mode or has_sample_query: ... sample_result = create_sample_fn(...) ... caption = sample_result.caption; lyrics = sample_result.lyrics; bpm = sample_result.bpm ...
if req.use_format and (caption or lyrics): ... format_result = format_sample_fn(...) ... caption = format_result.caption or caption; lyrics = format_result.lyrics or lyrics

skip_lm_tasks = {"cover", "cover-nofsq", "repaint", "extract"} ... need_lm_for_cot = params.use_cot_caption or params.use_cot_language or params.use_cot_metas; use_lm = (params.thinking or need_lm_for_cot) and llm_handler ...
infer_type = "llm_dit" if need_audio_codes and params.thinking else "dit"
use_cot_metas=not sample_mode,
has_all_metas: 'bpm' and 'keyscale' and 'timesignature' and 'duration' ... if not has_all_metas and use_cot_metas: (Phase 1 CoT)
thinking: bool = False ... sample_mode: bool = False ... use_format: bool = ... default=False ... use_cot_caption: bool = True; use_cot_language: bool = True
parse_description_hints: regex language mapping + 'instrumental'/'pure music'/'solo' detection

claims.topic: (b) Plan-only 'Enhance' API exists as separate endpoints
claims.claim: Yes — the LM plan/rewrite is obtainable WITHOUT committing to generation, via three mechanisms: (1) POST /format_input — synchronous; takes prompt, lyrics, temperature, param_obj{duration,bpm,key,time_signature,language}; runs format_sample and returns {caption, lyrics, bpm, key_scale, time_signature, duration, vocal_language}. This is the natural 'Enhance' button backend. (2) POST /v1/create_sample — synchronous; takes query, instrumental, vocal_language, temperature; explicitly documented as 'the API equivalent of the Gradio UI's Simple Mode Create Sample button'; returns full plan {caption, lyrics, bpm, keyscale, duration, timesignature, vocal_language}. (3) POST /release_task with analysis_only=true — async job that runs only the LM CoT phase (infer_type='dit', no DiT, no audio) and returns metas + LM caption with lyrics echoed back. Returned plans are plain values the client can feed into a later /release_task; there is no server-side plan state. Both synchronous endpoints lazy-init the LM and honor token auth (verify_token_from_request).

evidence.quote: @app.post("/format_input") ... """Format prompt/lyrics via LLM and return normalized metadata fields.""" ... return wrap_response({"caption": ..., "lyrics": ..., "bpm": ..., "key_scale": ..., "time_signature": ..., "duration": ..., "vocal_language": ...})
@app.post("/v1/create_sample") ... This is the API equivalent of the Gradio UI's Simple Mode "Create Sample" button.
if req.analysis_only: lm_res = llm_handler.generate_with_stop_condition(..., infer_type="dit", ...) ... "generation_info": "Analysis Only Mode Complete" ... "lyrics": params.lyrics
register_sample_format_routes(...) ... register_release_task_route(...)

claims.topic: (c) User-supplied lyrics pass verbatim into DiT conditioning
claims.claim:
In the default request path (sample_mode=false, sample_query empty, use_format=false) user lyrics are NEVER rewritten, filtered, or sanitized: req.lyrics -> PreparedLlmInputs.lyrics -> GenerationParams.lyrics -> dit_input_lyrics, which is never reassigned from LM output (the LM-metadata merge only fills lyrics when the user's are empty, and the CoT phase does not emit lyrics; use_cot_lyrics is explicitly 'not consumed by inference'). The DiT receives them wrapped only as '# Languages
{language}

# Lyric
{lyrics}<|endoftext|>' and tokenized with truncation at max_length=2048 tokens — the sole transformation. There is no strict/verbatim flag because verbatim is the only behavior absent opt-in rewrites. Caveats: (1) use_format=true DOES rewrite lyrics via the LM ('Format the user's input...' instruction; empty lyrics become '[Instrumental]'); (2) sample_mode/sample_query REPLACES lyrics wholesale; (3) when thinking=true the lyrics additionally condition the LM's audio-code generation prompt, but the DiT text conditioning still uses the user's verbatim lyrics; (4) the CAPTION is NOT verbatim by default — use_cot_caption defaults to true and replaces dit_input_caption with the LM CoT caption, so a verbatim-caption client must send use_cot_caption=false; (5) lyrics equal to '[inst]'/'[instrumental]' (or empty) set the instrumental flag.

evidence.quote: caption = req.prompt; lyrics = req.lyrics ... original_lyrics = req.lyrics or ""
dit_input_lyrics = params.lyrics ... dit_generate_kwargs = {"captions": dit_input_caption, ..., "lyrics": dit_input_lyrics,

if not caption and metadata.get('caption'): caption = metadata.get('caption'); if not lyrics and metadata.get('lyrics'): lyrics = metadata.get('lyrics')  (fill-only-when-empty; result not routed to dit_input_lyrics)
if params.use_cot_caption: dit_input_caption = lm_generated_metadata.get("caption", dit_input_caption)  (caption rewritten; no lyrics equivalent exists)
use_cot_lyrics: bool = False  # gate is read in cli.py; not consumed by inference

def _format_lyrics(self, lyrics: str, language: str) -> str: return f"# Languages
{language}

# Lyric
{lyrics}<|endoftext|>"
lyrics_text = self._format_lyrics(lyrics[i], actual_language); lyrics_inputs_dict = self.text_tokenizer(lyrics_text, padding="longest", truncation=True, max_length=2048, ...)
format_sample_from_input: if not lyrics or not lyrics.strip(): lyrics = "[Instrumental]"
is_instrumental: lyrics_clean in ("[inst]", "[instrumental]")

claims.topic: (d) No safety tuning or content filtering in the request path
claims.claim: A case-insensitive grep for refus/sanitiz/nsfw/safety/explicit/profanit/moderat/censor/blocklist/banned across acestep/ finds zero content-safety logic — all hits are memory-safety margins, thread-safety locks, filesystem path_safety, or the word 'explicit' meaning 'explicitly specified'. The four LM system prompts are single-line task instructions with no safety language ('Generate audio semantic tokens...', 'Understand the given musical conditions...', 'Expand the user's input...', 'Format the user's input...'). Constrained decoding is a purely structural FSM (yaml field order, BPM 30-300, duration 10-600s, valid keyscales/languages/time signatures, audio codes 0-63999) with no lexical or content constraints on caption/lyrics values. parse_lm_output does no filtering. The only request-path gating is optional API-key/token auth and audio-path validation. Note: this covers serving code only — whether the LM weights themselves were safety-tuned cannot be determined from this repo.

evidence.quote: DEFAULT_LM_INSTRUCTION = "Generate audio semantic tokens based on the given conditions:" ... DEFAULT_LM_REWRITE_INSTRUCTION = "Format the user's input into a more detailed and specific musical description:"
FSM ... Enforces structured metadata generation (BPM, duration, keyscale, etc.) ... Validates numeric ranges (BPM: 30-300, Duration: 10-600s) ... MAX_AUDIO_CODE = 63999
parse_lm_output: regex extraction of <|audio_code_N|> and yaml fields only; no filtering
api_key = os.getenv("ACESTEP_API_KEY", None); set_api_key(api_key)

claims.topic: (e) LM inference settings and REST exposure
claims.claim: REST-exposed on /release_task (JSON, form, or multipart; aliases per PARAM_ALIASES): lm_temperature (default 0.85 via LM_DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE), lm_cfg_scale (default 2.5; applied only in the codes phase — the CoT phase hard-forces cfg_scale=1.0), lm_top_k (None/0 = disabled), lm_top_p (default 0.9; >=1.0 disables), lm_negative_prompt (default sentinel 'NO USER INPUT', used as the CFG unconditional prompt), lm_model_path and lm_backend (effective only at lazy init), allow_lm_batch (alias 'parallel_thinking'), constrained_decoding_debug. DEAD FIELDS: lm_repetition_penalty is parsed into the request model but never consumed in the API path, and constrained_decoding is parsed but build_generation_setup hardcodes use_constrained_decoding=True. Max tokens is NOT exposed: it is computed from target duration (codes phase: duration*5 codes + 10; CoT: +500 buffer; capped at max_model_len-64 where max_model_len = 4096, or 2048 in low-VRAM vllm mode). 'Thinking' is not a chat-template toggle: the <think>...</think> CoT block is structural and always used; REST `thinking` instead selects llm_dit (LM generates audio codes). Phase 1 always stops at '</think>'. The plan-only endpoints expose only `temperature` (default 0.85); /format_input and /v1/create_sample do not accept top_k/top_p over HTTP. understand/full-analysis uses fixed temperature 0.3.

evidence.quote: LM_DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE = 0.85; LM_DEFAULT_CFG_SCALE = 2.5; LM_DEFAULT_TOP_P = 0.9

lm_model_path ... lm_backend: Literal["vllm", "pt", "mlx"] = "vllm" ... lm_temperature: float = 0.85; lm_cfg_scale: float = 2.5; lm_top_k: Optional[int] = None; lm_top_p: Optional[float] = 0.9; lm_repetition_penalty: float = 1.0; lm_negative_prompt: str = "NO USER INPUT"
CoT phase cfg: "cfg_scale": 1.0  # CFG must not be applied during CoT (text generation) phase

thinking=thinking, lm_temperature=req.lm_temperature, lm_cfg_scale=req.lm_cfg_scale, lm_top_k=lm_top_k, lm_top_p=lm_top_p, lm_negative_prompt=req.lm_negative_prompt, ... use_constrained_decoding=True,  (hardcoded; req.constrained_decoding ignored)
_compute_max_new_tokens: target_codes = int(effective_duration * 5); codes phase target_codes + 10; CoT target_codes + 500; min(..., self.max_model_len - 64)
STOP_REASONING_TAG = "</think>" ... stop_at_reasoning=True,  # Always stop at </think> in Phase 1
PARAM_ALIASES: "sample_query": ["sample_query", "sampleQuery", "description", "desc"], "allow_lm_batch": [..., "parallel_thinking"], "prompt": ["prompt", "caption"]
temperature = to_float(body.get("temperature"), 0.85)  (only sampling knob on plan-only endpoints)
understand_audio_from_codes(audio_codes=..., temperature=0.3, ...)

claims.topic: Job flow context for the TS client
claims.claim: All generation goes through POST /release_task (queued, single worker) -> POST /query_result polling; the worker path executes run_blocking_generate which calls prepare_llm_generation_inputs (LM pre-steps) then generate_music. The OpenRouter-compatible POST /v1/chat/completions on the same server converts to the identical GenerateMusicRequest (temperature->lm_temperature, thinking passthrough, lyrics field bypasses message parsing), so it adds no new LM semantics. Default API port is 8001 (ACESTEP_API_PORT), CORS allows localhost origins only.

evidence.quote: _run_one_job ... return run_blocking_generate(app_state=app.state, req=req, ... create_sample_fn=create_sample, format_sample_fn=format_sample, generate_music_fn=generate_music, ...)
prepared_inputs = prepare_llm_generation_inputs(...) ... generation_setup = build_generation_setup(...) ... analysis_result = maybe_handle_analysis_only_modes(...)

return GenerateMusicRequest(prompt=prompt, lyrics=resolved_lyrics, ... lm_temperature=req.temperature if req.temperature is not None else 0.85, ... thinking=req.thinking if req.thinking is not None else False,
--port default int(os.getenv("ACESTEP_API_PORT", "8001"))
CORSMiddleware allow_origins ["null", "http://localhost", "http://127.0.0.1"] + localhost regex

open_questions: The checkout is not a git repo (per environment), so the v0.1.8 tag pin could not be verified against git metadata; line numbers cited are from the working tree at /Users/zathras/WAVESMITH_Project/engine/ACE-Step-1.5.

Whether the acestep-5Hz-lm weights themselves received safety/RLHF tuning is unknowable from serving code; only the absence of serving-side filtering is established. Empirically test with explicit lyrics during M0 ground-truth rendering.

Edge case: inference.py:804 reads lm_generated_metadata.get("caption") inside the use_lm branch; if the LM returns empty metadata (all_metadata_list empty -> lm_generated_metadata=None) with use_cot_caption=true this would raise AttributeError — worth a defensive try/catch expectation in the client (job would fail with error).

The /format_input and /v1/create_sample endpoints hold an app-level LLM init lock but the LM generate call itself is outside the job queue — concurrent Enhance calls during a running generation share the same LLMHandler; nano-vllm has an internal generate() lock but pt/mlx paths may contend. Serialize Enhance calls client-side to be safe.
constrained_decoding and lm_repetition_penalty request fields are accepted but dead in v0.1.8 — do not surface them in the TS client.

verdicts.claim: LM model identity: 'acestep-5Hz-lm' family in 0.6B/1.7B/4B; 0.6B and 4B are standalone ACE-Step/* HF repos, 1.7B is bundled in ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5 and is DEFAULT_LM_MODEL; Qwen3-style chat model; loaded from <checkpoints>/<lm_model_path>.

evidence.quote: LM_MODEL_NAMES = {"0.6B": "acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B", "1.7B": "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B", "4B": "acestep-5Hz-lm-4B"}

MAIN_MODEL_REPO = "ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5" ... "acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B": "ACE-Step/acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B", "acestep-5Hz-lm-4B": "ACE-Step/acestep-5Hz-lm-4B" ... MAIN_MODEL_COMPONENTS = [..., "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B"] ... DEFAULT_LM_MODEL = "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B"
if lm_model_path is None: lm_model_path = "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B" ... full_lm_model_path = os.path.join(checkpoint_dir, lm_model_path)
because Qwen's chat template renders assistant messages containing a </think> tag
"architectures": ["Qwen3Model"] (bundled checkpoint in working tree corroborates Qwen3)

verdicts.claim: Backend selection: valid backends {vllm, pt, mlx}; mlx (or vllm-on-MPS) attempts MLX first with PyTorch fallback; vllm on non-CUDA becomes pt; vllm falls back to pt on preflight warning or free VRAM < 2GB, and to MLX-then-pt if vllm init throws on MPS; MPS config sets recommended_backend='mlx' and lm_backend_restriction='pt_mlx_only'.
verdicts.correction: Everything checks out EXCEPT the 'MLX-then-pt if vllm init throws on MPS' fallback: that code (llm_inference.py:753-759) is unreachable. With backend='vllm' and device='mps', line 678 routes to the MLX attempt first; if that fails, line 705-709 rewrites backend to 'pt' for any non-CUDA device BEFORE _initialize_5hz_lm_vllm can run, and _initialize_5hz_lm_vllm itself bails when CUDA is unavailable (line 792-795). So the vllm-failure branch can only execute with device='cuda', where the `device == "mps"` condition at line 754 is always false. The actual MPS sequence for requested 'vllm' is: try MLX (if available) -> rewrite to pt -> pt load.

evidence.quote: VALID_LM_BACKENDS = {"vllm", "pt", "mlx"}
if backend == "mlx" or (backend == "vllm" and device == "mps"): ... # else: backend was "vllm" on MPS, continue to vllm attempt below ... if backend == "vllm" and device != "cuda": ... backend = "pt"
VRAM_SAFE_FREE_GB = 2.0 ... if device == "cuda" and free_gb < VRAM_SAFE_FREE_GB: ... falling back to PyTorch backend
if device == "mps" and self._is_mlx_available(): (inside the vllm-failure branch, only reachable when device == "cuda" — dead code)
lm_backend_restriction="pt_mlx_only" if _mps ... recommended_backend="mlx" if _mps

verdicts.claim: ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND semantics: startup resolves via resolve_lm_backend (invalid/missing -> recommended_backend, then restrictions); per-request lazy init precedence is req.lm_backend > ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND > 'vllm'; /format_input and /v1/create_sample read only the env var; no --lm-backend CLI flag.
verdicts.correction: The stated per-request precedence is the literal code expression, but it is misleading: GenerateMusicRequest.lm_backend is a non-optional Literal defaulting to 'vllm' (release_task_models.py:109) and build_generate_music_request always fills it with parser.str('lm_backend', 'vllm') (release_task_request_builder.py:87), so req.lm_backend is ALWAYS truthy and ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND is never consulted on the /release_task lazy-init path (llm_readiness.py:64). The env var only matters at startup init and on /format_input//v1/create_sample//reinitialize. Also note the per-request path validates only against the literal set and does NOT apply resolve_lm_backend's GPU restrictions (pt_only/pt_mlx_only); non-CUDA handling happens later inside LLMHandler.initialize. To get mlx on a Mac via lazy init, a client must explicitly send lm_backend='mlx' or rely on the vllm+mps auto-MLX path in LLMHandler.initialize.

evidence.quote: lm_backend = resolve_lm_backend(os.getenv("ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND"), gpu_config)

verdicts.claim: (a) LM trigger matrix on /release_task: required by thinking/sample_mode/sample_query/use_format/full_analysis_only; optional via use_cot_caption/use_cot_language (default true, auto-disabled); sample_mode replaces prompt/lyrics/metas wholesale with hint parsing; use_format rewrites caption+lyrics; generate_music runs CoT for every text2music request when LM loaded except cover/cover-nofsq/repaint/extract and when all four metas supplied; thinking=true (REST default false) switches to llm_dit.
verdicts.correction: Three omissions: (1) text2music with flow_edit_morph=true ALSO skips the LM entirely (inference.py:622-633: morph_on_text2music treated like a skip task), so the exception list is incomplete; (2) the CoT phase runs not just for text2music but for any non-skip task — lego and complete also engage the LM when loaded (skip set is only {cover, cover-nofsq, repaint, extract} plus the morph case); (3) thinking=true yields infer_type='llm_dit' only when the user has NOT supplied audio_code_string — `infer_type = "llm_dit" if need_audio_codes and params.thinking else "dit"` where need_audio_codes = not user_provided_audio_codes (inference.py:564-570,684). Everything else verified, including use_cot_metas=not sample_mode (job_generation_setup.py:197) and the has_all_metas skip (llm_inference.py:1245-1251,1367).

verdicts.claim: (d) No safety tuning or content filtering in the request path: greps find zero content-safety logic; LM system prompts are single-line task instructions; constrained decoding is a purely structural FSM (BPM 30-300, duration 10-600, keyscales/languages/timesigs, audio codes 0-63999) with no lexical constraints; parse_lm_output does no filtering; only gating is API-key/token auth and audio-path validation.
verdicts.correction: One precision worth adding (not an error): the FSM's effective duration ceiling at runtime is GPU-tier dependent — the processor is constructed with max_duration=gpu_config.max_duration_with_lm (480s on most tiers, 240s tier1, 600s only on 'unlimited'), so 10-600 is the absolute bound, not the effective one (llm_inference.py:621-631).

parse_lm_output: regex extraction of <|audio_code_N|> and yaml-ish fields only; no filtering

verdicts.claim: (e) LM settings REST exposure: lm_temperature 0.85, lm_cfg_scale 2.5 (codes phase only; CoT forces 1.0), lm_top_k None/0=disabled, lm_top_p 0.9 (>=1.0 disables), lm_negative_prompt 'NO USER INPUT' sentinel, lm_model_path/lm_backend effective only at lazy init, allow_lm_batch (alias parallel_thinking), constrained_decoding_debug; lm_repetition_penalty and constrained_decoding are dead; max tokens duration-derived (codes: dur*5+10, CoT: +500, cap max_model_len-64, 4096 or 2048 low-VRAM vllm); <think> CoT structural, Phase 1 stops at </think>; plan endpoints expose only temperature; understand uses fixed 0.3.
verdicts.correction: Two errors: (1) lm_top_p >= 1.0 does NOT disable top-p over REST — prepare_llm_generation_inputs normalizes any falsy or >=1.0 value back to 0.9 (`lm_top_p = req.lm_top_p if req.lm_top_p and req.lm_top_p < 1.0 else 0.9`, llm_generation_inputs.py:65), so the GenerationParams-level 'None if >= 1.0' disable branch (inference.py:649) is unreachable from the API; top-p is always active in (0,1) for /release_task. (2) allow_lm_batch is a third dead field in the REST path: it is parsed into GenerationConfig.allow_lm_batch (job_generation_setup.py:206) but generate_music never reads it (only docstring/field-def hits in inference.py:231,244); the API path always chunk-batches via lm_batch_chunk_size=8 (inference.py:687). It is consumed only by Gradio UI code. Also worth noting: with thinking=false (REST default), lm_cfg_scale and lm_negative_prompt are inert since CFG applies only in the never-run Phase-2 codes generation. All other points verified, including the 0.85/2.5/0.9 defaults (api_server.py:115-117), CoT cfg_scale=1.0 (llm_inference.py:1379-1391), token math (llm_inference.py:266-303), max_model_len 4096/2048 (llm_inference.py:62,819-822), and temperature-only plan endpoints (sample_format_routes.py:107,183).

verdicts.claim: Job flow: POST /release_task (queued, single worker) -> POST /query_result polling; worker runs run_blocking_generate -> prepare_llm_generation_inputs -> generate_music; /v1/chat/completions converts to the identical GenerateMusicRequest adding no new LM semantics; default port 8001; CORS localhost-only.
verdicts.correction: Structure, port, and CORS confirmed; two precision fixes: (1) worker count defaults to 1 but is env-configurable via ACESTEP_QUEUE_WORKERS (api_server.py:195); uvicorn itself is pinned to workers=1 (server_cli.py:90-96). (2) The OpenRouter adapter does NOT use identical defaults: lm_cfg_scale defaults to 2.0 there (openrouter_models.py:109) vs 2.5 on /release_task, and batch_size defaults to 1 (openrouter_adapter.py:389) vs 2 on /release_task (job_generation_setup.py:203). The conversion mechanics (temperature->lm_temperature 0.85 fallback, thinking passthrough default False, lyrics field bypassing message parsing) are confirmed.

missed: Response envelope: /release_task, /query_result, /format_input and /v1/create_sample all wrap payloads as {data, code, error, timestamp, extra} (acestep/api_server.py:120-128). Critically, LM generation failures on the plan endpoints come back as HTTP 200 with body code=500 (sample_format_routes.py:144-146, 234-235); only LM init failures raise real HTTP 500/503. A TS client must check body.code, not just HTTP status.

analysis_only is NOT in the require_llm set (llm_generation_inputs.py:87). If the LM is uninitialized/disabled, run_blocking_generate passes llm_handler=None (job_blocking_generation.py:129-130) and the analysis_only branch calls None.generate_with_stop_condition (job_analysis_runtime.py:84-85), so the job fails with an AttributeError rather than a clean 'LM required' message.

On MPS, cover/cover-nofsq requests proactively UNLOAD the LM before generation to free unified memory (llm_generation_inputs.py:75-85: llm_handler.unload(); app_state._llm_initialized = False). The next LM-needing request triggers lazy re-init — relevant latency/behavior on the target Mac deployment.

Lazy init never retries after a failure: ensure_llm_ready_for_request returns immediately when _llm_init_error is already set (llm_readiness.py:34-37), so one transient init failure permanently disables LM features (use_cot_* silently off, LM-required requests erroring) until POST /reinitialize or process restart. POST /reinitialize (reinitialize_route.py:63-65) can reload the LLM using env settings.

batch_size on /release_task defaults to 2 when omitted (job_generation_setup.py:203), so a single-song client must send batch_size=1. GPU-tier max_batch_size_with_lm/without_lm values exist in gpu_config (gpu_config.py:316-470) but are NOT enforced anywhere in the API request path (only printed at startup, startup_model_init.py:199-200).

The constrained-decoding duration ceiling is GPU-dependent: the FSM processor is built with max_duration = gpu_config.max_duration_with_lm (llm_inference.py:621-631) — 480s on tiers 2-6b, 240s tier1, 600s 'unlimited' — and _compute_max_new_tokens clamps target duration to the same ceiling (llm_inference.py:270-278). Requesting duration above the tier ceiling is silently clamped in the LM path.

sample_mode with an empty sample_query substitutes the literal query 'NO USER INPUT' (llm_generation_inputs.py:119), and req.vocal_language overrides the regex-parsed language hint only when it is not 'en'/'unknown'/'' (llm_generation_inputs.py:122-125) — i.e., the REST default vocal_language='en' is ignored in sample mode, so an English-language constraint must be inferred from the query text.

is_format_caption is a third parsed-but-dead REST field (release_task_models.py:115, release_task_request_builder.py:98): no consumer anywhere in the API path — like lm_repetition_penalty and constrained_decoding, it should not be surfaced in the TS client.

Additional LM env knobs the analyst did not list: ACESTEP_LM_DEVICE (defaults to handler device at startup, 'auto' on lazy init) and ACESTEP_LM_OFFLOAD_TO_CPU (startup_llm_init.py:75-77; llm_readiness.py:75-76; sample_format_routes.py:90-91). Also ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR / ACESTEP_PROJECT_ROOT control where <checkpoints> resolves (model_downloader.py:329-359) — though llm_readiness/sample_format_routes hardcode os.path.join(project_root, 'checkpoints').

GenerationParams.thinking defaults to True at the dataclass level (inference.py:192) while the REST model defaults to False (release_task_models.py:27) — only the REST default matters for the HTTP client, but anyone calling generate_music directly inherits LM-codes mode by default.

full_analysis_only requires src_audio_path: it first encodes the source audio to codes via dit_handler.convert_src_audio_to_codes and fails the job if that errors (job_analysis_runtime.py:54-58) before running understand_audio_from_codes at fixed temperature 0.3 — it is an audio-analysis endpoint, not a text-plan endpoint.

On MPS the LM is kept in float32 for the pt backend (NaN/inf risk in fp16; llm_inference.py:566-583), and Phase-2 codes generation builds its prompt from caption+lyrics+CoT via build_formatted_prompt_with_cot with the training-aligned open assistant turn '<think>...</think>\n\n' (llm_inference.py:1485-1490, 1783-1800) — the user's lyrics condition LM code generation whenever thinking=true.
